content: Chickens and pigs are monogastric animals that cannot convert polyunsaturated fatty acids (PUFAs) into saturated fatty acids.
When fed an evolutionarily-inappropriate diet high in PUFAs, these animals accumulate them in their fat tissue.
Most pigs and chickens are raised on diets high in corn and soy, which are sources of PUFAs.
Ruminants like cattle, deer, and bison are raised on grass, keeping PUFAs in their fat at less than 2%.
Historically, human ancestors consumed meat and organs from large, grazing ruminants, suggesting an optimal diet for humans may include lower PUFA levels.
Avoiding species with high metal content in fish, such as tuna and swordfish, is recommended on an animal-based diet.
